rules.condition: [Patch rule — melanoma_vs_melanocytic_nevus] Lesion has a darker central core that transitions smoothly and gradually to lighter brown toward the periphery (centrifugal pigment gradient); Border is soft, blurred, or feathered — not angulated, notched, or sharply demarcated in focal areas; No blue-white structures, shiny white lines, or regression areas are present; No radial streaming, pseudopods, or peripheral globules indicating asymmetric growth; No atypical pigment network with thick irregular lines and irregular holes; Background pigment network, if visible, is relatively regular or blended/homogeneous; Lesion is roughly symmetric in overall shape and pigment distribution despite appearing heterogeneous at first glance
rules.action: Classify as Melanocytic Nevus. Confidence: medium. Rule: When a lesion displays a central darker zone that fades gradually and centrifugally into lighter brown at the periphery without sharp color cutoffs, and the overall border is soft/feathered rather than angulated, and there are no blue-white structures, regression areas, atypical network, or radial streaming, classify as Melanocytic Nevus.

lineage.reason: patch rule authored by expert VLM to correct o4-mini failure

rules.condition: [Patch rule — basal_cell_carcinoma_vs_benign_keratosis] Fine fingerprint-like or gyrate ridges visible within the lesion body; Admixture of light brown and pinkish-grey tones without true steel-blue or blue-white ovoid nests; Grey areas are diffuse and smudged rather than forming discrete blue-grey globules or ovoid nests; Absence of arborizing (branching) telangiectasia on close inspection; Absence of leaf-like structures or spoke-wheel areas at the periphery; Border shows gradual fading or moth-eaten edges rather than sharp well-defined margin typical of BCC
rules.action: Classify as Benign Keratosis. Confidence: medium. Rule: When a lesion displays a central structureless zone with admixed brown and pinkish-grey hues, fingerprint-like or ridge-like fine linear structures radiating within the lesion, and a gradual moth-eaten or smudged border without arborizing telangiectasia, leaf-like structures, or spoke-wheel areas, classify as Benign Keratosis.

lineage.reason: patch rule authored by expert VLM to correct o4-mini failure

rules.condition: [Patch rule — actinic_keratosis_vs_benign_keratosis] Flat pigmented lesion with a mixture of brown and grey-blue hues in a patchy distribution; Fingerprint-like or cerebriform parallel ridge structures visible within the pigmented area; Grey-blue structureless zone present centrally, suggesting regression or lichenoid inflammation; Relatively well-circumscribed overall border without ill-defined erythematous spread into surrounding skin; Absence of surface scale, strawberry pattern, or rosettes typical of actinic keratosis; No prominent surrounding erythema or diffuse pink structureless background extending beyond the lesion
rules.action: Classify as Benign Keratosis. Confidence: medium. Rule: When a flat pigmented lesion shows a central zone with multiple colors (brown, grey-blue, pink) arranged in a patchy/mottled pattern with fingerprint-like or cerebriform ridges visible within the pigmented area, and the lesion has a well-demarcated border with a sharp transition from pigmented zone to surrounding skin, classify as Benign Keratosis (solar lentigo / lichen planus-like keratosis).
